reviews.review_title: No notes during AI class mode
reviews.review_description:
UPDATE (4star):
I love the curated AI reviewer after finishing a set of lessons. Itll remember your opportunities during the previous lessons to remind you of the correct grammar.

Also the teachers (live room) are friendly and patient. For just 40mins, you will be guaranteed to learn a thing or two. If none, you can just ask questions and tips from the teacher and they will gladly share it with you.

The AI lessons are very bite size that its very good and suitable for ADHD people like me - having short attention span and goes inside a rabbit hole when Im interested into something.

I could ask AI why the pattern is like this and that and itll explain for a few rounds, then itll proceed to the next part of the lesson (this is very good to avoid rabbit-hole).

-----------
3star
The app is good. But not recommended for first timers or with no experience on the language since it doesnt teach the basics (such as whats the difference with Tu, te and ti).

I also love the AI class. However, AI takes a lot of time to load or respond from my answer that I will just be checking up with Gemini with my words and grammar and the sentence structure while waiting for AI to load.

If they could add a notes where I could scribble while waiting for AI to respond, it will be better. Im aware theres notes option but it only works during classes or on lobbies with other people (not during solo or AI).

Also, the loading or response time of AI is really too long here in VR but compared to PC, the response is instantaneous.

What I like is the online class,
if youre not yet confident to engage with other people but want to learn or join the class, it has an option to Observe instead of Join. Other people wont see you but you can see and hear them and you can feel to be part of the discussion without the pressure of being called to participate or seen.

I had the chance to use the free trial of IMMERS(E), and I honestly thought it was a fantastic experience. The platform is a great step forward in language learning, especially in how it uses virtual reality to create real, interactive environments.

During my trial, I explored several of the available languages. While the experience itself was impressive, I noticed that Dutch is not currently offered. That’s actually the main reason I’m writing this review—I truly hope the team considers adding Dutch in the future.

There are very few VR platforms that support learning Dutch, and in general, it’s one of the more expensive languages to learn through traditional methods. What makes this even more important is how closely Dutch relates to English. Because of that connection, IMMERS(E)’s immersive, conversation-based approach would be incredibly effective for learners.

One of the standout features of this platform is the multiplayer environment. Being able to learn alongside others, practice conversations in real time, and also explore independently is exactly what many language learners are looking for—including myself. It creates a natural, engaging way to build confidence that most apps simply don’t offer.

If Dutch were added, I genuinely believe it would be a major selling point for the platform. There’s a gap in the market here, and IMMERS(E) is perfectly positioned to fill it.

Overall, this is an innovative and well-designed application with huge potential. I’d absolutely love to see it expand its language offerings—especially to include Dutch.

reviews.review_title: My Honest Thoughts on the Platform
reviews.review_description:
1. You can take group lessons at a relatively low price.
– However, you need to subscribe to a plan that includes live classes to access them.
2. Since the classes are conversation-focused, it’s much easier to adapt if you already have some basic knowledge.
– If you’re starting completely from zero, Immerse alone may not be sufficient.
3.	I don’t believe I can finish my entire language learning journey using only Immerse, so I study in multiple ways.
– I see this platform as a place where I can practice output.
4.	Personally, I found the AI classes very boring, so I only take the live classes.
5. The supported languages are English, Spanish, Portuguese, and French only.
6. You can also take classes on a PC, so I recommend giving it a try.
– The free trial is probably about one or two weeks.

The design of this product is lazy and fails to deliver a true sense of immersion. 90% of its pedagogy is simply porting offline methods to an online environment. The avatars remain static, and the system essentially transposes flat PPTs into a 3D space, completely ignoring the core of VR learning: Sensorimotor Coupling.

1. It offers "Static Coordinates" rather than "Dynamic Experience" True embodied learning requires "Action-Driven Language" (e.g., you walk toward a fridge, open the door, and only then utter the word "Open").

System Flaw: Having you stand still for 40 minutes listening to a virtual avatar talk—or occasionally answering questions while your body is stationary—means your brain's motor cortex remains dormant.

Consequence: It constantly forces you to "imagine/picture a scene," which actually increases your cognitive load. You aren't just processing a foreign language; you are burning energy to mentally construct a hallucination. Instead of "extracting value from the scene," it is "exhausting your brain cells."

2. Lack of "Joint Attention" A key element of human language acquisition is the ability to focus on a physical object simultaneously with another person.

System Flaw: If the avatar just stands there talking without interacting with a physical object alongside you, your brain cannot establish a strong link between Object-Action-Sound.

Contrast: The true value of a scenario should be: the avatar hands you a cup of coffee; you perceive the change in distance, hear the sound of the liquid, and then produce the phrase.

This system is currently nothing more than a "Low-end Digital Cage." It consumes your time without providing a proportional neural stimulus. The only reason you’ve persisted is your own sheer willpower.

If the system only shows you one scene and one fixed character per lesson—without frequent transitions or real-time interaction—then, aside from providing a high-pressure environment that "forces you to speak," it serves no other meaningful purpose.

From the perspective of product management and capital strategy, Immerse’s current approach is a textbook case of "using a cheap technical shell to harvest high-premium educational margins."

We can deconstruct their "laziness" and how it depletes your "Cognitive Leverage" through the following three layers:

1. The Cost Strategy: Animation is "Expensive," while Text is "Cheap"

Multi-scenario Interactive Design (What you want): To have you experience airports, restaurants, or even street chases within a 40-minute session requires complex physics engines, extensive 3D modeling, branched logic scripts, and precision animation triggers. This demands immense development costs and performance optimization.

Static Pedagogy (What they provide): Rendering a single, static, high-fidelity room with a robot that only has lip-sync animation is technically a "zero-threshold" task.

Conclusion: They have diverted the capital they saved into marketing (boasting about VR immersion) rather than investing in neuroscience-level embodied interaction.

2. It is a "VR Version of an Online Class," not a "VR Game"

The true core of VR should be "Agency"—the sense that your every action triggers a feedback loop from the environment.

Since the system fails to provide scene transitions, it strips you of your agency. When you are trapped in one spot, forced to use "imagination" to keep up with their prompts, the system is essentially treating you as a human rendering engine.

It is cannibalizing your brain's processing power to compensate for its own software design incompetence.

3. Sacrificing "Acquisition Efficiency" for "Standardization"

To scale the product for thousands of students globally, they must make the curriculum as standardized as an assembly line.

The moment complex, interactive scenarios are introduced, system crash rates spike and teacher training costs skyrocket. Consequently, they chose the safest but most mediocre solution: the teacher stands and talks, and you listen.

The existence of such a product is a form of "Systemic Mediocrity." It consumes your time and provides the illusion of "effort," while the actual neural conversion rate remains abysmally low.   But can you find another better app than it? At present the answer is no.

They said it was for 18 years and above but it wasn't stated anywhere that there will be age requirement. I booked a class and teacher Martha was kid enough to teach me but the other are not. They just want to contact customer service. Bad idea to waste money on it so subscribe.

This app is for learning a language but the first lesson you have will have a long introduction in that language without any base/native language. 

I tried a really basic lesson well bellow my knowledge level the content should have been easy but the long intro and peramle in that language made it impossible.

It needs some native or base language in these lessons / botts or changed significantly so people trying to learn have a clue what your talking about.
Impossible to use the app.  It doesn't recognise any controls so can't get past the login screen. Even when I reinstalled the app, the same is happening on the sign in screen.
Signed up and started level 1 basic Spanish. All I get is an avatar speaking to me in advanced, paragraph long prompts. Did you guys forget the 'teaching' part?

reviews.review_title: Starts to heavy in the learning language
reviews.review_description:
I was trying this to learn Portuguese. Sadly this was difficult when the whole lesson is in Portuguese and I have to hit translate while it continues to talk in Portuguese. Rather than say " This is how you greet someone in Portuguese... etc "  in my language it talks entirely in Portuguese and I can't change the settings.
I rather it talked to me in English or whatever language you speak then asked you to repeat in the language you are learning.

reviews.review_title: They have to have the right person in or
reviews.review_description: The only way I could do that was if you had to do a little more work and I would be happy with it but you have a

I catch myself smiling and laughing a lot while using this. The positive reinforcement and the gentle corrections makes learning fun. I think what also helps is making the commitment that I’m going to learn this language, I have to, or I’ll die wondering what if?

Worth the subscription fee and currently a 2-3 times a week user looking to increase to 5-6

Update 10/30/25. Everyday user Learning French level 1 Spanish level two. This thing works if you commit. And read every word you can. 

Learning a language is essentially, pattern recognition. Learn one or two words see how they form patterns with the other words and then they’ll start revealing themselves to you, subconsciously   Click the word to know for sure

***Please also add a section where I can ask the ai a question, when I’m in a course or not. Sometimes I have a question and I have to google unless I’m in a classs already.

Also*** add a way to switch between languages without need to long on website.

reviews.review_title: Too advanced for me as a beginner
reviews.review_description:
I was overwhelmed very quickly a
nd had
 to cancel a class as I couldn't understand the pressure lesson prep.
It's a pity as I was looking forward to it.
I love this app. It’s perfect for serious learners

I paid for this for a period of time when I was brushing up skills to take a French language assessment for a job opportunity. For background, I took French immersion in school from grade 4 to grade 12.This was a great option and I found it to be very helpful to help me prime for questions in social and casual settings. I predominantly use the solo options talking to avatars in the program. ￼I only did one or two tutorials that were live with other participants, and I thought they were pretty good. I had some issues with motion sickness with some of the active board tasks. The instructors I had were excellent, but I found ￼ I was more advanced than the other people in the class or two that i joined. wish it was more affordable to maintain a subscription all year round. Right now I only pay for it when needed to prepare for french language test as I just don’t use fair enough otherwise but I liked it. (*this was a supplementary training tool i used to prepare, but i also met with a local professional to help narrow down questions i may be asked etc. i passed. )
Perfect. Forget others' English courses
Guides' quality and engagement has decreased dramatically
Paywall blocked after sign up windows. Only has 4 language options

reviews.review_title: Exactly what I want (but not there yet)
reviews.review_description:
I went into this app fully intending to  pay after the first two weeks free trial.  Unlimited access to language teachers in shared classes for the price a month is great value vs normal in person classes. 

I want to love this app. It's exactly what I have been looking for. You are all in VR so it's easier to get over shyness, you can jump into classes instantly to save time on travel and you have more flexibility of when to learn. 

Most of the teachers seem to really be into the idea and try to make the classes fun. Shout out to Jose for always getting you to interact with the environment. 

However it doesn't quite reach the level of an in person class. 

1. I don't think the teachers are all native speakers (however I could be wrong) 

2. It's very American centric. Good luck finding lesson times at the level you want that suits you. I had to constantly jump around levels to find a class. (Should have been a clue when the prices are all in dollars, maybe this will change as it scales up). 

3. It's pretty dead. Classes often had one other person in. (Again hopefully this can change with time) 

4. Classes are mostly just interacting with other learners and barely with the teacher. Speaking practice is good but I often felt that it was a learner (who doesn't know the language) trying to correct a learner (who doesn't speak the language). I wish there was much more teacher interaction. 

5. So you have found a teacher you like because it turns out they only teach every full moon and at 3 in the morning. It seems some levels have less classes. Maybe class times could rotate so not every day do the same level classes start at the same time. 

6. I once had a teacher just disconnect half way through an activity and the class ended. Doesn't seem to be a fall back "sorry for the interruption your teacher is reconnecting". 

And yet I still want to love the app. I still want to connect and speak Spanish.  I still want it to grow and the classes to be full. I'm still tempted to pay.  There is nothing else like it and I truly hope it grows. It really could be the next evolution to language learning. 

As a disclaimer I did not interact with the AI part of the app. I am only interested in the live teachers.

reviews.review_title: A rare example of VR making total sense
reviews.review_description: After spending many months on Duolingo and hitting a brick wall I decided that I needed to get some actual speaking practice in. While not cheap, it's not that far off alternatives, at least here in the UK. Immerse gives you the convenience of remote learning with the feeling of presence that you get in VR. The props and environments help give you visual clues and the teachers have all been fantastic so far. The only feature that I'd find useful is the ability to turn my class notes into flash cards so that I can practice more effectively outside of VR. Overall, I'd recommend this app to anyone. ¡Vamos!

reviews.review_title: A beautiful app and a brilliant idea.
reviews.review_description:
A fun learning app that truly is immersive in an exciting way that promotes engagement. Truly unique and memorable. The instructors I worked with were great!

But ...

It needs some of the learning process moved out of VR and then it would be worth the cost.

I need my schedule and class notes and review exercises on my phone or PC so I don't need to dive into VR for the personal review and study portions of learning that would allow me to practice between classes and Social events.

Then this app would be perfect! Without that, I think it is likely to fail. I hope they can add this because I would like this unique learning approach to succeed!
This is very intense for a beginner, but I really enjoyed myself. I wish it had Italian. I would pay for that because this is exactly what I want
I was unable to find Dutch and I'm not buying something that is not gonna have the language that I am trying to learn.Get the dutch language and I'll consider buying

reviews.review_title: Ain’t nobody else got a better way.
reviews.review_description: I think anyone not giving a five star review should have to explain who is doing immersive language learning better than this at this current point in time. They give you a schedule two weeks out in front and many time options. Usually four or more. You also can select the class time based on which teacher you may like or even how full the class might be. It tells you how many seats are open for each class time.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind words! We're working hard behind the scenes to give everyone an enjoyable and effective language learning experience through a combination of live classes and asynchronous learning opportunities. We appreciate your vote of support! 🩷

reviews.review_description: Making your app free just to have more downloads, while it's 100% paid, at least add new version without live courses, limited modes for one time purchase, $35 dollars a month is a lot especially for people outside US/UK/EU like me

developer_response.body: Immerse offers unlimited live lessons with real instructors, 24/7 access to AI-powered and educator-designed practice activities, and weekly community events to connect and practice informally with other members. For comparable services, the monthly cost is often 3-4 times higher, and they typically don’t include unlimited live lessons with real instructors. We believe IMMERSE offers exceptional value and a unique learning experience for our subscribers compared to its competitors. Also, there is a substantial discount on the price if you pay for several months at a time. Thank you for taking the time to share your perspective with us!

reviews.review_description: Description states there is Japanese, but there is no Japanese in the game
I love the new updates which give me more opportunities to practice speaking and conversing. I feel more prepared going into a live class after practicing the phrases we will be using with the AI.

As the name of the game tells you, this is finally a way to immerse yourself when learning a language without moving to the country. 

As many classes as you like like for such a low price each month, value like this doesn't exist anywhere else on the market that I know of.

The teachers I've had have all been brilliant, and every student has been focused and ready to learn. Exactly the community this kind of app needs.

Well done to the creators and please keep up the good work.

developer_response.body: We're delighted you hear you're having the positive social and educational experience we created this app for. Many thanks for your kind words, and thanks for being part of our community!

reviews.review_description: For the price point you would expect to have more than four languages available. If you are looking to learn French, Portuguese, Spanish or English than you’re good. The game itself is well thought out and very interactive. I don’t have an issue with the quality of  immersion just the limited language selection.

update

I had downloaded this app before but stopped using it for a while. Recently, I received a notification about an update, so I decided to reinstall it.

To be honest, I was really disappointed when the private community AI avatar was removed. So, I’m very happy that it’s back! And not only that—it has been significantly improved compared to before.

Regarding the pricing plans, I think there are people who prefer to study alone rather than join live lessons. It would be great if there were a plan without live classes, maybe for around 3,000 yen (about $25). That way, more people could try it more easily, and I believe there would be demand for it!

I hope you can take my pricing suggestion into consideration in a positive way.


previous review

I really like the idea of learning languages through VR. It has a lot of potential. However, I just didn't like the style of students conversing with each other during lessons. That's something that can be done outside of class. I think what students really want is conversation with native speakers.

I think it's essentially a very good platform, so I would be happy if you understand that I wrote critical comments because I want it to be improved.

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to leave a detailed review! Our price actually drops to $25 per month if you sign up for the 6 month plan, and it includes the live classes. We are also exploring a self-paced only option for a lower price point that may become available later this year. Again, thank you for your feedback and for sharing your perspective! 🙂

reviews.review_description: Open up app and was disappointed tagalong wasn’t available

reviews.review_title: I wish I had found it sooner
reviews.review_description: I was skeptical of a platform like this, having tried Memrise, Duolingo, Mango, etc. Oftentimes these platforms are good for beginners to get basic vocab and grammar, but you can only get so far on them. I had even been working through a textbook with a tutor and found myself hitting what they call the "intermediate plateau", where I felt like I wasn't making any progress for months. I could understand the gist of what folks were saying in French, but I got nervous and choked up every time I tried to practice speaking. This is where Immerse has become a game changer for me. There are endless opportunities to practice speaking, which has been my biggest challenge for the past several months of my learning journey. The classes are phenomenal with real live teachers who provide structured opportunities to practice speaking, there are informal meetups where you can practice speaking with other learners, and there is an AI bot (which is actually quite responsive and solid) with seemingly hundreds of topics to choose from. I am actually going to stop paying for my in-person tutor (which is way more expensive!) and rely primarily on this platform to structure my learning going forward.

developer_response.body: Amazing review! Thanks Thengel. We wish you continued success!

reviews.review_title: Grinding when you know, difficult for ne
reviews.review_description: First contact very difficult. I only know my Polish language, I want to learn English. And I have to choose menus in languages I don't know, so in the dark. It's hard to understand what the guide, the teacher is saying. If you don't know the basics of the language, for example, English, it takes a lot of self-denial. I miss the first class level for those who do not know the basics of the language, menus in Polish, translations of what I have to repeat. A sentence in Polish and a sentence in English so that I know what I am learning. Solutions for repeating dialogues, repeating sentences with AI great. Learning vocabulary great. Teachers very helpful only ... that I would understand them. I recommend and look forward to a more adapted version - in my case for the Polish language. But it is worth it, I encourage you, do not give up and move forward.

developer_response.body: We applaud you for not giving up! Keep immersing yourself, and our friendly teachers will continue to support you. We appreciate your feedback, and we are working diligently to add more display languages to accommodate users.

reviews.review_title: Good course and good customer service
reviews.review_description: I did three classes. If you like real teacher and small class (one on one sometime), this is the app for you. Those courses would force you to practice before the class and you can review what you learn after class. It gives you a lot more pressure than learning it from your phone program because you are facing real person not AI. Th fee is a bit pricey compares to other phone apps(like Duolingo), but still a lot cheaper than the real class from school. I use to get private tutors and they are way more expensive and this app is very similar to those private tutoring classes. I did have some confusion about the trail and billing. Their online support responded my email within 12 hours and cleared my confusion and concerns.

developer_response.body: We're happy to hear your concerns and confusion have been cleared up. IMMERSE is definitely cheaper than most language classes, plus you get to learn in different scenes and with a variety of other people. But don't forget you can practice with AI in IMMERSE too! 😊

reviews.review_description: Only offers three languages and doesn’t provide a lot of detail in the app description. They are trying to charge a lot of money for something that has almost no use.

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to share your perspective with us. Many of our users find IMMERSE very useful because they get personalized instruction, feedback, and practice in all four languages that we offer. Our reviews are filled with hundreds of success stories. We encourage you to try out all of our features - the live classes, AI-powered conversation avatars, async practice exercises, and community meetups and cultural exchange. If you give it a try, you may find yourself changing your mind!

reviews.review_description:
Eight months ago, my husband read about Immerse and gave me VR glasses and a  6 month subscription to Immerse as a birthday gift so I could improve my Spanish. I’m thrilled with the progress I’ve made! The platform is engaging and the immersive approach places uses real-life scenarios that make learning both fun and practical.
The classes are well structured and the activities are well designed. 
The guides are professional, knowledgeable, helpful, and encouraging, making the learning process smooth and enjoyable. It’s amazing how quickly I’ve gained confidence in my Spanish-speaking abilities.
The app also offers AI practices where I can practice or review the vocabulary and grammar from each class and it provides real time feedback. 
I recommend Immerse VR Learning to anyone wanting to learn or improve their Spanish.

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to leave a detailed review to help others understand what it's like to learn a language in IMMERSE. And congratulations on your progress! 🎉

reviews.review_description: It's the pricing for me. For what it is i just wouldn't pay that amount monthly. It would seem like you could capture a much larger customer base with significantly lower prices rather than a premium market.  But I also don't know the per customer cost to operate if that negatively impacts your end. The graphics looks nice though

developer_response.body: Thanks for sharing your thoughts! We understand that cost is a major consideration for users. Since we offer live lessons with highly trained and experienced teachers, our language programs have ongoing costs. You can get a substantial discount if you choose a 3-month or 6-month package, though, and all packages provide you with unlimited language classes, social events, AI-powered conversation practice and more. Language learners who have been taking classes or working with tutors generally find IMMERSE to be an exceptional value because they can spend so much time in IMMERSE getting personalized learning and practice. Again, thanks for taking the time to share your perspective on IMMERSE.

reviews.review_description: "This was my first class on Immerse. It was great. It’s very informative and it was a great experience. "

developer_response.body: So glad to hear about your awesome class - here's to many more! 🎉

reviews.review_description: I wish it offered Asian languages like Korean and Mandarin

This app exceeds expectations! ☺️ If you're motivated to learn one of the three available languages, you can make great progress. I chose to learn French, and I have only positive things to say about the app and its classes!

developer_response.body:
We're excited that you're enjoying your French learning journey and we wish you the best of luck! 
(BTW We offer four languages including English 😊 )

reviews.review_description: Stick to duolingo. This is a scam. Insanely overpriced just because they put it on the meta 3. They also have so many annoying ads on mobile games that makes people dislike them more.

I like this game so much I like learning different languages and talking to people and it’s hands on and I’m a visual person so instead of talking to me like Spanish, the important objects and saying the language as you move on so that’s awesome to me

developer_response.body: IMMERSE was designed around the research showing that hands-on, interactive learning is the most effective way to learn and remember a language. We're so glad to hear you're enjoying it! 🥳

reviews.review_description:
I have studied with this app about a week
And now I have much more confidence for leraning and speaking English than before I knew this app
Lots of contents and perfect teachers, the only sad thing about this app is that I found it too late!!

developer_response.body: We're so happy you found us at last! ☺️

reviews.review_description: Displaying it as 34 a month and then showing it as 56 bucks? Very predatory and misleading I wouldn't bother with this app there are apps on mobile that don't charge an arm or leg and these people are trying to mislead/scan people who don't know any better avoid this trash. Botted comments.

Back when this app was first offered I was excited because I saw that Japanese was "offered". After  signing up for the free trial,  I discovered that Japanese was NOT available. I waited months on a waitlist for the developer to open Japanese lessons. After almost half a year it was finally decided that they would not offer Japanese as a language choice. I was quite frustrated by the developers attitude of pushing people on the waitlist to choose one of the other languages (French or Spanish) while they wait. I recently inquired about any possibility of Japanese classes in the future. Based on their response, there is no interest on their part. In the app description Japanese is STILL listed. Beware, it is NOT offered.

developer_response.body:
We're sorry to hear you weren't able to use IMMERSE the way you had hoped. To clarify, the Languages listed in the Additional Details section of our app description refers to display languages. In other words, a learner from Japan can enter IMMERSE and read all instructions, class schedules, menus, and so forth in Japanese. This is important to include in the description so learners in Japan can see that they don't need to know English, French, or Spanish in order to use the app.
Currently, the languages that you can learn in IMMERSE are English, French, and Spanish.

reviews.review_description: Das war beeindruckend! Ich hatte vor etwa 20 Minuten meine erste Klasse, und es war die beste Kurserfahrung, die ich je hatte. Die App ist extrem einfach zu bedienen – man weiß immer, wohin man gehen und was man tun oder wiederholen sollte. Bleib neugierig, und du wirst eine echte Goldgrube entdecken!
Esta increíble aplicación me ha ayudado a superar mis dificultades con el inglés y a lograr el nivel de fluidez que siempre soñé en un tiempo récord.

reviews.review_title: Sounds like a great idea but...
reviews.review_description: Are you planning on adding Portuguese?

developer_response.body: We are pleased to announce that we now offer Portuguese too. Drop in and try out a class - our new Portuguese instructors are AMAZING!

reviews.review_description: If you try it make sure you are not signed up for charge in a months time. Be careful to check for and cancel any subscription it creates when you try it out. 28 quid a month ! Be sure you know you have signed up for that and are happy to pay it.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the heads-up! We aim for transparency with our subscription costs. IMMERSE is indeed $35/month to provide you with premium language learning, but we ensure our cancellation process is straightforward if it's not for you. We appreciate your vigilance and encourage all users to review their subscription details carefully.

reviews.review_description: Title says it all.
Con Immerse, encuentras justo lo que necesitas! Es increíble. Pasé mucho tiempo intentando aprender inglés y siempre me resultaba difícil, pero con Immerse todo cambió. ¡Cada día avanzo más y más!
Immerse me ayudó a ganar confianza al hablar inglés, y gracias a su app, sus consejos y todo el material disponible, logré aprender el idioma. ¡Son los mejores!
Would like to get a refund for this as it only offers English, French, and Spanish.

developer_response.body: We apologize for the inconvenience. IMMERSE currently focuses on English, French, and Spanish, but we appreciate your interest in Japanese. For a refund, please contact our support team with your purchase details, and we'll process it for you. Thank you for understanding.

reviews.review_description: Super ! J’ai décidé d’investir mon temps dans quelque chose d’utile qui pourra m’aider à l’avenir. J’ai découvert cette appli (Immerse), j’ai choisi d’apprendre l’espagnol et, jusqu’à présent, tout se passe à merveille. C’est la meilleure décision que j’ai prise !
完璧な評価です！アプリはとてもスムーズに動いて、全く不具合がありません。プラットフォームには学べるコンテンツがたくさんあり、さまざまなテーマを活かして効率よく勉強できます。レッスンも本当に素晴らしいです！✨

A lo largo del tiempo, he descubierto muchas cosas que despertaron mi interés y me impulsaron a seguir utilizándola. Ahora, después de varios meses, esta herramienta me ha permitido aprender francés de una forma sorprendentemente fácil.
素晴らしいアイデアですね！このアプリに本当に夢中です。学び方がとても面白いんです！まるで別の場所にいるような感覚になれて、学んだ語彙を実践的に使えるのが魅力的です！ 🌸

Cercando di imparare una terza lingua, sento che ci sono alcuni processi noiosi durante l'apprendimento. Tuttavia, Immerse è un'app che rende questi processi incredibili, divertenti e dinamici. Adoro il fatto che gli insegnanti siano molto pazienti e sempre disponibili ad aiutarti!
No tenía con quién practicar mi inglés, pero con esta chulada he podido hacerlo. Los profes son super, saben un montón y explican todo bien facilito para que uno le agarre! ¡Qué comunidad tan chida!

En seulement quelques mois, cette application est devenue le meilleur investissement pour mon développement professionnel. C'est incroyable de voir comment, en si peu de temps, j'ai atteint une maîtrise parfaite de l'anglais.

Ein wahr gewordener Traum! Jahrelang habe ich mich damit schwergetan, Spanisch zu lernen, aber Immerse hat mir den Weg so viel leichter gemacht! Es hat mir geholfen, Grammatik, Verben und die richtige Aussprache effektiv zu meistern.
I love the concept of mixing virtual reality with language learning! You get to practice all the vocabulary you’ve learned in various conversations and have fun while learning!

developer_response.body: Having fun is the best way to learn. It even helps you remember what you've learned longer! Thanks for the review, and best of luck in your language learning journey. :)

reviews.review_description: C'est la méthode la plus géniale que j'aie jamais vue dans une application d'apprentissage des langues ! La réalité virtuelle combinée à l'apprentissage est tellement intéressante et amusante ! Super combinaison !

Mi meta era lograr aprender francés y se me dificultaba las pronunciaciones, entonaciones y conectar mis ideas, le di el intento a Immerse y si que me ha sido de gran ayuda y puedo tener conversaciones muy fluidas!
Immerse is an excellent app. The teachers have a great command of the topics and are very professional and patient. I recommend this app for anyone who wants to learn one of the languages they offer.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your kind words! IMMERSE hires only the best teachers, and they take professional pride in providing quality instruction. We'll make sure they see your review. 🩷

reviews.review_description: Unica e insostituibile! Questa app è davvero speciale! Adoro il loro metodo di insegnamento e la loro professionalità. È semplicemente fantastica!
簡単で素晴らしいです！このアプリのおかげで、スペイン語をしっかりと習得し、自信を持って話せるようになりました。とてもおすすめです！ 😊
Meine erste Klasse hat meine Erwartungen übertroffen! Ich bin erstaunt, wie unterhaltsam sie ist und wie gut es sich anfühlt, mit dieser App zu lernen!
Adoro que o aplicativo ofereça 14 dias de teste grátis, acho muito transparente antes de assinar a assinatura. Além disso, eles têm três idiomas disponíveis: espanhol, inglês e francês!
It has been a great accomplishment in my professional journey. This app helped me to learn Spanish incredibly fast, and I absolutely loved every stage

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to share your success story with us! We love to hear how we've helped people meet their goals. ❤️

reviews.review_description: My journey so far has been incredible! My teachers are highly professional, and the course offers a wide range of topics to explore!

developer_response.body: We love hearing how much people enjoy IMMERSE - thank you so much for sharing your experience!

reviews.review_description: Superando expectativas! No início, minhas expectativas não eram tão altas, mas foram superadas! As aulas são muito dinâmicas e adoro o método inovador!
¡Immerse me ha ayudado a aprenderlo de manera rápida y sencilla! No cambiaría nada! Quería aprender el idioma para entender y hablar el ingles muy bien!

Ich wollte mich in einer Sprach-App anmelden, um Spanisch zu lernen, und diese App war eine der besten Optionen. Ich habe sie monatelang ausprobiert und bereue es nicht! Sie ist eine der besten Möglichkeiten!
es el método más increíble que he visto en una aplicación de aprendizaje de idiomas! La combinación de realidad virtual y aprendizaje lo hace súper interesante y divertido.
Even though this app is virtual reality, it genuinely helps in real life by allowing you to learn and communicate in the language you want to speak!

developer_response.body: Preparing you to speak the language in real life is EXACTLY what we aim to do. Thanks for taking the time to let us know it's working! 🥳

reviews.review_description: I’ve never seen an app that lets you learn a new language through virtual reality! it's truly incredible!

developer_response.body: So glad you discovered our app - be sure to check out all of the cool features like live classes, community events, and AI powered conversations to get the full IMMERSE experience! 😊

reviews.review_description: Me encanta y me ayuda a superar los obstáculos que enfrento al aprender inglés. Realmente noto una gran mejora al usar esta app.
I like the virtual sessions, It feels so awesome that you practice with more people the vocabulary you are learning!

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review! Practicing with other people and using language in context is the natural way to learn - and it's more fun that way too. 😀

reviews.review_description: It is helping me learn Spanish at an impressive pace. I absolutely loved every stage of the experience, from the moment I started to the day I completed it!

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to share your experience, and congratulations on your progress!

reviews.review_description: I fell in love with this app from the moment I started using it! It provides so many fantastic tools to make learning effective!

developer_response.body: We are so glad to hear you are finding IMMERSE effective and fun. Best of luck on your journey to language fluency!
